commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c7ebb42c30ebaa79f9bbabd88eca457bc8b2be6a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the native Anthropic provider and one provider test so completed prior assistant turns omit signed thinking blocks while the active tool-use turn keeps its thinking signature.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests -7. Total -2 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main and v2026.6.9 still have Anthropic replay paths that preserve signed thinking when replayThinkingEnabled is true, and the linked issue includes production 400-loop evidence. I did not run live Anthropic traffic in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] There is one concrete mechanical blocker: mirror the completed-turn stripping rule and regression coverage into the managed Anthropic transport.

target.title: fix(anthropic): strip thinking blocks from completed prior assistant turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94228: on the native Anthropic provider path, long-lived multi-turn tool-use sessions eventually brick permanently with HTTP 400 `"Invalid signature in thinking block"`. The same stored history is replayed on every retry, so the thread can never recover.

## Root cause

The Anthropic request-builder re-emits **every** stored thinking block from **all** prior assistant turns on every request, with its persisted signature. On long threads, the replayed multi-turn structure no longer matches the per-turn context each signature was minted against. Anthropic guidance states thinking blocks from already-closed turns may be omitted on replay.

## Changes

In `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`:

1. **Always compute the active tool-turn assistant index** (previously only computed when `replayThinkingEnabled` was `false`)
2. **Strip thinking blocks from non-active turns only** — the active tool-use cycle always keeps its thinking for tool-result signature continuity

```diff
- const activeToolTurnAssistantIndex = replayThinkingEnabled
-     ? -1
-     : findActiveAnthropicToolTurnAssistantIndex(transformedMessages);
+ const activeToolTurnAssistantIndex =
+     findActiveAnthropicToolTurnAssistantIndex(transformedMessages);
```

```diff
- if (!replayThinkingEnabled && i !== activeToolTurnAssistantIndex) {
+ // Strip thinking from completed prior turns; keep active tool-turn thinking
+ if (i !== activeToolTurnAssistantIndex) {
```

### v2 fix: corrected regression from v1

The initial version used `!replayThinkingEnabled || i !== activeToolTurnAssistantIndex` which incorrectly stripped ALL thinking blocks when `replayThinkingEnabled` was `false`, including the active tool-use cycle. The corrected condition `i !== activeToolTurnAssistantIndex` preserves active-tool-turn thinking for both `replayThinkingEnabled` modes:
- `true`: strips completed-turn thinking (fix for #94228) ✓
- `false`: strips completed-turn thinking, keeps active tool turn ✓ (preserves original contract)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Long multi-turn Anthropic tool-use sessions brick with HTTP 400 `"Invalid signature in thinking block"` because all historical thinking blocks with stale signatures are replayed. Per reporter: ~11 occurrences/24h across multiple long threads after upgrading to 2026.6.6.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24, full repository checkout
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```shell
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts --run
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  ✓ src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts (35 tests) 35 passed
  ```
  All 35 anthropic provider tests pass. Key invariants covered:
  - Active tool-use cycle: thinking blocks with signatures preserved (needed for tool-result continuity)
  - Completed prior turns: thinking blocks stripped, replaced with `[assistant reasoning omitted]`
  - No tool calls scenario: all thinking from prior turns stripped
  - `replayThinkingEnabled: false`: active tool-turn thinking preserved (original contract, no regression)
- **Observed result after fix**: Stale thinking signatures from completed turns are no longer replayed. Only the active tool-use cycle's thinking is preserved, preventing the signature-invalidation 400 on long threads consistent with Anthropic's documented guidance.
- **What was not tested**: Live multi-turn Anthropic session with 10+ tool-use cycles (requires API access and extended session)

## Related

- #84002: same failure pattern on OpenAI-responses path (reasoning-replay poison loop)
- #92373: merged PR that established the active-tool-turn preservation helper reused here

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
